# f-flat-minor (F♭m)

f-flat-minor is a minimal stack-oriented programming language where the only data type is big integers. Programs push and transform values on a stack, can define words, and can print output through built-in operations.

## Headless execution via URL

This web app supports automatic execution from a URL query parameter:

- Use `?code=...` with one of these prefixes:
  - `txt.` for URL-encoded source text
  - `b64.` for URL-safe base64 source
  - `c64.` for deflate-compressed + URL-safe base64 source
  - `bc.` for raw FF bytecode (base64 VLQ), decoded to FF-compatible IR source
- Raw source in `?code=` is not supported.
- On page load, the app decodes `code` and fills the editor; use **Run Program** to compile and execute.
- The URL query parameter updates only when code is run.

## DOM landmarks for agents

After each run, read the following:

- `#output` for normal execution output (stdout/diagnostics/stack details shown in output view).
- `#error` for error text (empty when there is no error).
- `document.body.dataset.ready` indicates run state:
  - `"false"` while execution is in progress.
  - `"true"` after execution has completed and DOM is updated.

## Example programs

- Program: `65 putc`
  - Expected output: `A`

- Program: `2 3 + putn 10 putc`
  - Expected output: `5` followed by a newline

- Program: `fact: dup 1 - [ dup 1 - fact * ] ? ; 6 fact putn`
  - Expected output: `720`

## Language reference

For full language details and project context, see the repository README:

- https://github.com/Hypercubed/f-flat-minor/blob/master/README.md
